title: Vortexa Freight Weekly
summary:
Main
By Dylan Simpson

ANALYSIS / EAST OF SUEZ / DIRTY
Flexibility and diversity keeping Suezmaxes afloat
Aframax and Suezmax utilisation for Russian crude
In the last few weeks, Suezmax utilisation for Russian crude has stagnated but remains high. In contrast, Aframax utilisation for Russian crude has declined almost 20% in the same period. This is currently driven by lower tonne-miles into East Asia for both tanker classes.

children.title: ANALYSIS / WEST OF SUEZ / DIRTY
children.summary:
VLCCs set to find support USG-to-Europe
Image reference: unnamed - 2023-06-29T124947.810.png | assets/2023-06-29_13s2kwa4isr51e418plw93b9u2es7s_img_unnamed-2023-06-29t124947810_6e7580210914.png
Weekly crude tanker utilisation USG-to-Europe by vessel class
Transatlantic crude tanker demand has been high as a result of Europe's multi-year high US crude imports . Aframax tonne-mile demand was especially high at the end of May on the back of the inclusion of WTI in the Brent pricing assessment.
